proposed_from.topic: Sharp uniform-in-DGP non-equivalence theorem between three leading double machine learning (DML) estimators for the average treatment effect under unconfoundedness: the original DML cross-fit estimator (Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins 2018, Econometrics Journal), the locally-robust orthogonal-score (LRO) estimator (Chernozhukov-Escanciano-Ichimura-Newey-Robins 2022, Econometrica), and the automatic debiased ML estimator (Chernozhukov-Newey-Singh 2022, Econometrica). All three target the same population functional E[Y(1)-Y(0)] and have first-order-equivalent influence functions when nuisance functions are estimated at the standard L^2 rate o(n^{-1/4}). The flagship question (axis b: non-equivalence frontier between three named published estimators): characterize the sharp boundary in nuisance-misspecification rate space r_g = r_g(n) for the outcome regression and r_p = r_p(n) for the propensity at which DML, LRO, and ADML have asymptotically equivalent variance vs. strictly different uniform-in-DGP variance lower bounds. The kernel claim is a closed-form spectral non-equivalence theorem: the three estimators achieve identical asymptotic variance bounds (and thus identical Wald confidence intervals at first order) if and only if r_g + r_p > rho_star, where rho_star is a sharp threshold function of the smallest eigenvalue of an explicit observable covariance kernel formed from the outcome residual and the propensity Riesz representer. When r_g + r_p <= rho_star, LRO and ADML strictly dominate DML by orthogonality, but LRO and ADML themselves diverge in variance lower bound proportional to the spectral gap, with ADML achieving the global semiparametric efficiency bound and LRO achieving only a local one. rho_star is a NEW mathematical object computable from observable second moments, not an existence/absence statement from prior work. Recovers the textbook L^2-rate equivalence (Chernozhukov et al 2018) at the boundary r_g+r_p=O(n^{-1/4}) and provides the first sharp triple non-equivalence boundary for DML estimators in closed form, separating the regime where DML, LRO, ADML give identical inference from the regime where the ordering DML < LRO < ADML is strict and quantifiable.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains banked eid_bjs_efficient_event_study, which used an estimator-frontier / EIF-gap discipline for staggered event-study estimators, and eid_lp_svar_nonequiv, which warned that strict non-equivalence cannot be assumed as a margin. The gap is that neither catalogue item concerns ExactID unconfounded ATE, DML/LRO/ADML variance rules, or weak-overlap studentization; this proposal imports the reviewer-requested common-EIF normalization and primitive-witness discipline into a different estimator family and estimand.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML, ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR, HongLeungLi2020, Dorn2025WeakOverlapDR, and MaSantAnnaSasakiUra2026WeakOverlapDR. The ε-gap is an iff frontier for when named DML/LRO/ADML point estimators remain equivalent but their published variance rules and Wald intervals separate under the same untrimmed ATE law; the closest weak-overlap DR papers repair or threshold estimators rather than classify triple studentization equivalence."}
seed_list: Prior work ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML establishes product-rate DML ATE validity; our seed conjectures that this equivalence fails uniformly below a spectral Riesz-overlap frontier because estimator-specific bilinear remainders survive even when the EIF is common.

Prior work ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO assumes locally robust moments remove first-step sensitivity; our seed conjectures that ATE-specialized LRO separates from ADML when the LRO Jacobian projection omits a residual Riesz direction.

Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML establishes automatic Riesz debiasing and robust standard errors; our seed conjectures that ADML is strictly variance-sharper than LRO on an open local misspecification class because its Riesz projection is global rather than moment-local.

Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR emphasizes Riesz instability under hard representer estimation; our seed conjectures that weak Riesz eigenvalues define the exact rate boundary where point-estimator equivalence ceases to imply Wald-interval equivalence.

Prior work AvagyanVansteelandt2022PBR permits valid ATE inference when one nuisance need not converge to truth; our seed conjectures that the triple DML-LRO-ADML boundary is non-rectangular because outcome and propensity errors share one spectral compatibility budget.

Prior work DukesVansteelandtWhitney2024DRDML notes nonregularity when only one nuisance is consistent; our seed conjectures that the nonregular region admits a three-estimator partition into common-validity, estimator-specific-validity, and common-failure regimes.

Prior work ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML would imply cross-fitting makes fold choices first-order irrelevant under product rates; our seed conjectures that in local weak-overlap sequences fold-independent cross-fitting still leaves a spectral variance gap across DML, LRO, and ADML.

Prior work ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO treats LRO as a broad semiparametric GMM construction; our seed conjectures a classification theorem where only invariant ATE moment/Jacobian normalizations collapse to the DML/ADML EIF.

Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML gives misspecification-robust standard errors; our seed conjectures that robust studentization, not the point estimate, is the first object to separate uniformly from DML under local-to-weak-overlap laws.

Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR would predict regularized Riesz representers stabilize global functionals; our seed conjectures a finite-support witness where the smallest positive covariance-kernel eigenvalue alone determines strict DML < LRO < ADML variance ordering.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML establishes product-rate DML ATE validity; our seed conjectures that this equivalence fails uniformly below a spectral Riesz-overlap frontier because estimator-specific bilinear remainders survive even when the EIF is common.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins
anchor_paper.claim: DML gives cross-fitted orthogonal-score ATE inference with uniform validity under nuisance-rate and overlap conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the seed predicts an iff spectral phase transition with strict variance gaps, not a routine product-rate restatement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML because DML already gives uniform validity under product-rate conditions.
seed_details.rebuttal: DML2018 does not compare DML, LRO, and ADML over one local DGP class or give pairwise variance lower bounds outside the product-rate equivalence region.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO assumes locally robust moments remove first-step sensitivity; our seed conjectures that ATE-specialized LRO separates from ADML when the LRO Jacobian projection omits a residual Riesz direction.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins
anchor_paper.claim: LRO constructs locally robust semiparametric moments by adding first-step influence adjustments and studies robustness of the resulting estimators.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/classification pattern, not blocked: the refuted object is a named published LRO workflow and the obstruction is an open class of omitted Riesz directions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from LRO because local robustness already characterizes the influence adjustment.
seed_details.rebuttal: LRO gives a general construction, not an ATE-specific classification of which moment/Jacobian normalizations collapse to the ADML Riesz projection.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML establishes automatic Riesz debiasing and robust standard errors; our seed conjectures that ADML is strictly variance-sharper than LRO on an open local misspecification class because its Riesz projection is global rather than moment-local.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh
anchor_paper.claim: ADML debiases regression functionals automatically using the function of interest, Riesz representation, and misspecification-robust standard errors.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism-decomposition pattern, not blocked: the seed isolates global-vs-local Riesz projection as a strict variance mechanism absent from the ADML theorem statement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ADML efficiency because ADML already reaches the semiparametric efficiency bound.
seed_details.rebuttal: ADML efficiency does not supply a uniform lower-bound comparison against a particular LRO specialization under local nuisance misspecification.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR emphasizes Riesz instability under hard representer estimation; our seed conjectures that weak Riesz eigenvalues define the exact rate boundary where point-estimator equivalence ceases to imply Wald-interval equivalence.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh
anchor_paper.claim: Regularized Riesz representers give orthogonal scores for global and local functionals and include ATE-adjacent debiased ML constructions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the seed turns Riesz instability into a sharp estimator-equivalence threshold with studentized consequences.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from regularized Riesz theory because weak representers already control variance.
seed_details.rebuttal: The Riesz paper does not derive a DML-LRO-ADML pairwise frontier or prove point-estimator equivalence can coexist with Wald-interval separation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AvagyanVansteelandt2022PBR permits valid ATE inference when one nuisance need not converge to truth; our seed conjectures that the triple DML-LRO-ADML boundary is non-rectangular because outcome and propensity errors share one spectral compatibility budget.

anchor_paper.author: Avagyan, Vansteelandt
anchor_paper.claim: The paper extends valid uniform ATE inference to cases where either the propensity or outcome estimator need not converge to truth.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Joint-misspecification pattern, not blocked: the seed predicts non-rectangular compatibility and estimator-specific gaps, not just weakening one nuisance consistency assumption.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from one-nuisance robustness because either nuisance may be inconsistent.
seed_details.rebuttal: One-nuisance robustness does not classify three named estimators over joint outcome-propensity spectral perturbations.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DukesVansteelandtWhitney2024DRDML notes nonregularity when only one nuisance is consistent; our seed conjectures that the nonregular region admits a three-estimator partition into common-validity, estimator-specific-validity, and common-failure regimes.

anchor_paper.author: Dukes, Vansteelandt, Whitney
anchor_paper.claim: The paper shows doubly robust DML inference in semiparametric regression but notes nonregularity can occur when only one nuisance is consistent.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the seed refines named nonregularity into a three-estimator partition with strict variance regions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DR-DML nonregularity results because they already identify the bad region.
seed_details.rebuttal: The JMLR paper does not compare DML, LRO, and ADML or give a spectral partition of pairwise variance lower bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML would imply cross-fitting makes fold choices first-order irrelevant under product rates; our seed conjectures that in local weak-overlap sequences fold-independent cross-fitting still leaves a spectral variance gap across DML, LRO, and ADML.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins
anchor_paper.claim: DML removes first-order regularization and overfitting bias when nuisance predictions satisfy product-rate conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment/process variation analogue, not blocked: the phenomenon is weak-overlap spectral survival after cross-fitting, not iid-to-Markov or fold bookkeeping.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from cross-fitting theory because fold independence already removes overfitting bias.
seed_details.rebuttal: Fold independence removes empirical overfit bias, not necessarily local Riesz-overlap variance separation across different orthogonalization schemes.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO treats LRO as a broad semiparametric GMM construction; our seed conjectures a classification theorem where only invariant ATE moment/Jacobian normalizations collapse to the DML/ADML EIF.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins
anchor_paper.claim: LRO is a general semiparametric GMM construction, not a classification of when its moments collapse to a particular ATE score.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Classification pattern, not blocked: the seed targets a named estimator workflow and a necessary-and-sufficient EIF normalization class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from semiparametric GMM invariance because moment normalizations are algebraic.
seed_details.rebuttal: The published LRO paper does not enumerate the ATE normalizations that produce the DML/ADML EIF or produce counterexamples outside that class.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML gives misspecification-robust standard errors; our seed conjectures that robust studentization, not the point estimate, is the first object to separate uniformly from DML under local-to-weak-overlap laws.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh
anchor_paper.claim: ADML provides misspecification-robust standard errors for debiased regression-function estimators but does not compare studentized ATE laws across DML/LRO/ADML under weak overlap.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target-variation pattern, not blocked: switching from point estimates to studentized laws reveals an inference gap hidden by common EIF centering.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from robust standard-error theory because ADML already states robust inference.
seed_details.rebuttal: ADML robust inference does not compare studentized statistics against DML and LRO over local-to-weak-overlap DGPs.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR would predict regularized Riesz representers stabilize global functionals; our seed conjectures a finite-support witness where the smallest positive covariance-kernel eigenvalue alone determines strict DML < LRO < ADML variance ordering.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh
anchor_paper.claim: Regularized Riesz representers give orthogonal scores for global and local functionals and include ATE-adjacent debiased ML constructions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/nonvacuity pattern, not blocked: the witness targets named estimators and is promoted to an open finite-support class rather than a degenerate instance.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Riesz eigenvalue calculations because finite-support kernels are just matrices.
seed_details.rebuttal: The matrix calculation is not in prior work and must connect three named estimator remainders to one observable covariance kernel and strict open-set ordering.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Chernozhukov et al. 2018, The Econometrics Journal, is the baseline DML ATE source: cross-fitting plus Neyman orthogonal scores gives uniform treatment-effect inference under product-rate and overlap conditions.
Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, and Robins 2022, Econometrica, gives the locally robust semiparametric GMM construction and the main general LRO comparator.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh 2022, Econometrica, gives ADML for causal and structural regression effects using automatic bias correction and robust standard errors.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh 2022, The Econometrics Journal, gives regularized Riesz representers for global and local parameters, the closest paper to direct Riesz-versus-propensity comparisons.
Newey and Robins 2018 studies cross-fitting and fast remainder rates, relevant to when fold/sample-splitting choices preserve first-order equivalence.
Avagyan and Vansteelandt 2022 extends uniform ATE inference to cases where one nuisance estimator need not converge to truth.
Dukes, Vansteelandt, and Whitney 2024 develops doubly robust inference for DML in semiparametric regression and explicitly notes possible nonregularity when only one nuisance is consistent.
Bruns-Smith, Dukes, Feller, and Ogburn 2024 connects augmented balancing weights, regression, and AutoDML-style estimators through exact algebraic equivalences and tuning-dependent behavior.
The main tension is that each estimator family has validity or equivalence statements in its own regime, but the literature does not classify uniform pairwise non-equivalence among DML, LRO, and ADML.
The strongest open substrate is exact-ID semiparametric ATE: nuisance-rate boundaries, EIF normalization, joint misspecification geometry, and weak-overlap studentization are all live gaps.

banked_reason: D0 re-test (post D0-solver upgrade) on prior kernel_substituted parent: D0 produced an honest artifact (split orchestrator settled all verdicts) but D0.5 reviewer judged the derivation below flagship — 'comparator frontier is not derived from the named published estimators and the generic-class witness is stipulated through structural matrices.' Intervention LLM hit JSON-parse failure 3x and fell back to route=user. Honest negative result: defect is upstream of D0 (proposal-level kernel/tier ceiling), not D0 solver.
